name: Karl Berger
description: German jazz vibraphonist, pianist and composer (1935–2023)
abstract: Karl Hans Berger (March 30, 1935 – April 9, 2023) was a German-American jazz pianist, vibraphonist, composer, and educator. He was a leading figure in jazz improvisation from the 1960s when he settled in the United States for life.

interviews.title: Karl Berger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

concerts.label: Creative Music Studio: Music of Frederic Rzewski

about_quotes.speaker: Ingrid Sertso
about_quotes.evidence: met Karl and then we wanted to just build something up together. He played piano in the band I worked in... And we went there, Karl and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ingrid Sertso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, August 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer shares a quote from Karl Berger, and the interviewee (Tom Varner) responds by discussing the content of the quote ('I like Karl's quote...'), indicating he is aware of Berger's views but no personal interaction or professional collaboration is described in the text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with The Creative Music Studio. The Creative Music Studio is something that was created by Karl Berger... In that period we got a chance to meet a lot of world renowned musicians... We all gave classes and things there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I just came and did some teaching with the students. And then we had Dave Holland, we had Colin Wolcott... So there were a lot of current musicians that were like on the faculty teaching there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: I just got another car with a great stereo system and I listened to two records that just kill me. A little known album by Karl Berger... I could listen to that forever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Marilyn Crispell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee responds to the interviewer's comment about Karl Berger but does not mention any personal interaction, collaboration, or direct relationship with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marilyn Crispell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 21, 2022
